@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9c80
+project: local-review
+title: 'feat(claude-agent): implement the session-fork ACP extension (claude --resume --fork-session)'
+---
+## What
+
+Companion to 01KTY8ZV79NX25KJ1BF4CTKPCR (llama-agent session fork): the SAME ACP fork extension, implemented by the claude backend, so the review fleet has ONE code path — prime → confirm → fork → prompt — with no backend branching. The fork contract (extension method / `_meta.forkFrom`, response reporting state attachment, `state_status`, pin) is defined by the llama task; this task makes crates/claude-agent honor it.
+
+Implementation sketch (verify against the actual claude CLI version and claude_process.rs):
+
+- [ ] Fork = spawn the child session's claude process with `--resume <parent-session-id> --fork-session` (the CLI's native conversation-fork primitive; `spawn_for_resume` in crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs is the precedent). Map the resulting CLI session to the new ACP session id; clone whatever agent-side session bookkeeping (conversation_manager/session.rs) the resume path already maintains.
+- [ ] `state_status` for a claude session reports saved=true once the parent's prime turn completed (state lives in the CLI's session files; there is no KV cache to evict) with promptTokens unknown/None — the contract must allow that. `pin` is a no-op that succeeds.
+- [ ] Fork response reports state attachment truthfully: if `--resume --fork-session` fails (unknown session, CLI too old), return the same distinguishable error the llama side returns so the fleet's fallback path is backend-agnostic.
+- [ ] Prefix-cache note (no code, verify + document): forks replay the identical first turn, so Anthropic's server-side prompt caching covers the shared prefix automatically. If claude-agent constructs API-level requests anywhere (rather than delegating to the CLI), confirm the prefix lands cache-eligible; do NOT build a custom caching layer.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Through the same pool client code that drives llama forks, a claude-backend session can be primed, forked N times, and each fork prompted independently — identical call sequence, no backend conditionals in the caller.
+- [ ] Fork of an unknown/expired parent fails with the same error shape as llama-agent's, and the fleet fallback path treats both identically.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] claude-agent unit tests with a fake/scripted claude process (the crate's existing process-test seam; <10s, no real CLI): fork spawns with `--resume <parent> --fork-session`; session bookkeeping cloned; unknown-parent error shape matches the contract; state_status/pin contract behavior.
+- [ ] Contract test shared with the llama implementation if a seam exists (acp-conformance is the natural home): both agents satisfy the same fork-extension conformance checks.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p claude-agent` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
+
+## Implementation Notes (2026-06-12)
+
+**Real-CLI verification first** (per task instruction): `claude --help` shows `--fork-session`; verified end-to-end against the installed CLI that `claude --print --resume <parent-uuid> --fork-session --session-id <child-uuid>` (a) is an accepted flag combination, (b) clones the parent's conversation (the fork answered with content only the parent's turn contained), and (c) lands the fork on the *specified* child UUID (a subsequent `--resume <child-uuid>` continued it). Also verified: a parent primed under `--no-session-persistence` CANNOT be resumed or forked ("No conversation found with session ID") — which drove a deliberate production change below.
+
+Shipped (all in crates/claude-agent, plus reuse of the committed wire contract in agent-client-protocol-extras::session_fork — no new wire types):
+
+- **`src/claude_process.rs`** — `SpawnConfig.fork_from: Option<SessionId>` (parent's agent-side id; precedence over `resume` — a fork IS a resume of the parent) and a private `ConversationAttachment` enum (`New` → `--session-id <uuid>`, `Resume` → `--resume <uuid>`, `Fork{parent_uuid}` → `--resume <parent-uuid> --fork-session --session-id <child-uuid>`) derived by `SpawnConfig::conversation_attachment()` and consumed by `build_base_command`. The child's CLI uuid stays the deterministic `SessionId::to_uuid_string` mapping, so forks are themselves resumable/forkable with no stored uuid map.
+- **`--no-session-persistence` moved from the always-on CLAUDE_CLI_ARGS to ephemeral mode only** (`configure_ephemeral_mode`, where every doc layer already said it belonged: "ephemeral = haiku + no session persistence"). This is REQUIRED for fork: the CLI can only fork/resume a transcript it persisted. Side effect: it also un-breaks the existing `session/resume`/`session/load` machinery in production, which re-spawns `--resume <uuid>` and could never have found a transcript while persistence was globally disabled. Durable sessions now write CLI transcripts under ~/.claude; ephemeral (validator-style) sessions still don't.
+- **`src/claude.rs`** — `ClaudeClient::fork_process(config)` beside `resume_process`: spawns the forked child process, no init trigger (same rationale as resume — it would inject a spurious turn), and detects the CLI's immediate-exit failure mode (parent transcript missing / CLI too old) via the shared `early_exit_stderr` helper (refactored out of `verify_resume_started`), surfacing stderr in the error.
+- **`src/session_fork.rs` (new)** — claude's side of the shared contract, mirroring llama's handler shape: `fork_session` (resolve parent → never-fork-blind gate → spawn forked CLI process FIRST (the fallible state-attach; no half-created child) → clone the in-memory `Session` under a fresh ULID (fresh timestamps, no title; context/cwd/MCP config carried) → wire transcript recorder + persist durable SessionRecord, the same lifecycle a completed turn gets). Error mapping matches llama exactly: clean miss / non-ULID id → `invalid_params` + `data.error=fork_parent_not_found` (permanent fallback signal); parent without a completed turn, agent in ephemeral mode, or CLI fork spawn failure → `fork_parent_state_unavailable`; session-store failure → retryable `-32603` internal error, never masquerading as not-found. `session_state_status`: `saved = has_first_exchange(session) && !ephemeral` (the existing first-user-message+first-agent-reply predicate, now shared), `promptTokens`/`bytes` = None (CLI exposes no counts; contract allows), `pinned` = false. `pin_session`: no-op success reporting effective state `pinned:false` (no eviction to pin against, per the contract's no-op allowance); unknown session → `session_state_not_found`. Response `prefix_tokens=None`, `state_attached=true`.
+- **`src/agent_trait_impl.rs`** — `ext_method` routes `session/fork` / `session/state_status` / `session/pin` (matched on the shared extras consts) to small handlers using the existing `parse_ext_params`/`to_ext_response` helpers; doc updated.
+- **Prefix-cache note (no code)**: verified claude-agent constructs no Anthropic-API requests anywhere — all generation goes through the spawned claude CLI, which manages server-side prompt caching itself; forks replay the identical prefix so caching applies automatically. Documented in the session_fork module doc.
+- **Drive-by bug fix (exposed by the new tests)**: `session_validation::validate_directory_permissions` "restored" the process CWD by reading `current_dir()` *after* already entering the probed directory — i.e. it permanently parked the process in every new session's cwd, so when a test TempDir cwd was deleted, unrelated tests failed to spawn `echo` (and in production the agent process would squat in session dirs). Fixed (capture original cwd before `set_current_dir`), with a `#[serial]` regression test watched red→green.
+- **Test support**: `#[cfg(test)] src/test_support.rs` with one RAII `StateDirGuard` (XDG_STATE_HOME isolation); the two inline copies in agent.rs tests migrated to it.
+
+**Shared acp-conformance fork test: deliberately not added.** A meaningful fork conformance check needs a *primed* parent (a completed prompt turn), which requires real backend generation (real Qwen model for llama, real claude CLI for claude) — the conformance crate's paired llama/claude integration fixtures already run minutes-long and a prime turn per backend would balloon them further, while the wire shapes are already pinned by unit tests in agent-client-protocol-extras and both agents' handler tests assert the identical error kinds/codes. The fleet task (01KTY91Y7AJRPJNBCVTV59HCJJ) is the natural end-to-end proof of the single client code path against both backends.
+
+TDD: every behavior red→green (the `--no-session-persistence` relocation and the CWD-restore fix were proper assertion-REDs against existing behavior; new-API tests failed-to-compile first). 7 session_fork handler tests + 5 claude_process arg/attachment tests + 1 session_validation regression test; lib suite runs in ~3s.
+
+Verification (fresh): `cargo test -p claude-agent -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p acp-conformance` — all green (claude-agent 733 lib + 317 integration + suites; extras 241+50; acp-conformance 177 unit + 125 integration). `cargo clippy -p claude-agent -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p acp-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean. claude-agent lib suite re-run twice more, stable 733/733.
\ No newline at end of file

+- [ ] `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs:1161` — Tests modified in this diff still hand-roll the flag-value lookup (`position(|a| a == flag)` then `args.get(pos + 1)`) that the newly added `arg_value` helper exists to do, so the same pattern now has both a helper and three inline reimplementations in one test module. Use `arg_value(&args, "--setting-sources")`, `arg_value(&args, "--input-format")`, and `arg_value(&args, "--output-format")` (with `.expect(...)`/`assert_eq!` as the assertions need) in `test_base_command_loads_filesystem_setting_sources` and `test_base_command_retains_core_streamjson_args`.
+- [ ] `crates/claude-agent/src/session_fork.rs:48` — `extension_error` is a generalization of the existing `ClaudeAgent::session_not_found_error` (warn + `invalid_params` + `{sessionId, error: <kind>}` data), but the existing function was left as a parallel special-case copy instead of being expressed through the new one — two shapes for the same uniform error contract. Move `extension_error` next to the other error constructors (e.g. `acp_error.rs`) and re-implement `session_not_found_error` as a one-line delegation to it, keeping a single canonical builder for the `{sessionId, error}` shape.
+- [ ] `crates/claude-agent/src/session_fork.rs:393` — The new test module names its tests without the `test_` prefix (`fork_clones_parent_history_under_fresh_id`, `fork_unknown_parent_errors_distinguishably`, `state_status_reports_truthfully`, `pin_is_noop_reporting_effective_state`, `ext_method_routes_fork_status_and_pin`), while the prevailing convention across this crate — including the tests added in this same change to claude_process.rs (`test_base_command_fork_attachment_args`, etc.) — is `test_`-prefixed test functions. The deviation also collides confusingly with the module's `test_config` helper, which carries the `test_` prefix despite not being a test. Rename the seven `#[tokio::test]` functions in session_fork.rs with the crate's `test_` prefix (e.g. `test_fork_clones_parent_history_under_fresh_id`), or if the unprefixed style is intended going forward, rename the `test_config` helper (e.g. to `headless_config`) so the prefix unambiguously marks tests.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs:1906` — `AcpServer::new_session` is a public method (the `session/new` ACP entry point) with no doc comment, while its modified siblings on the same surface (`ext_method`, `register_session`, `persist_session_record`) all gained rustdoc in this change — leaving the primary session-creation API the only undocumented entry point. Add a rustdoc comment summarizing the lifecycle it drives: validates MCP transports, creates the llama session with the request cwd, injects the default mode's system prompt, mounts the mandatory in-process Agent-tools server (failing the request if the mount fails), attaches external MCP clients with tool discovery/routing, then registers the session via `register_session` and returns the response with mode state.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs:3167` — `require_capability` takes two adjacent same-typed parameters (`undeclared_message: impl Into<String>`, `uninitialized_message: impl Into<String>`) whose meanings differ only by position. A call site that swaps them compiles silently and reports the wrong diagnostic to the client. Group the two messages into a small struct (e.g. `CapabilityErrorMessages { undeclared, uninitialized }`) or pass a single closure `FnOnce(CapabilityFailure) -> String` keyed by an enum, so the compiler distinguishes the two cases.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs:3870` — The test model config hardcodes `batch_size: 512` six times across duplicated test-server builders. A behaviour-configuring size literal repeated this often invites drift — changing the test batch size means finding and editing six call sites, and a reader cannot tell whether 512 is load-bearing or arbitrary. Introduce a single `const TEST_BATCH_SIZE: u32 = 512;` (or better, one shared `test_model_config()` helper the six builders call) so the value is named once.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/session_fork.rs:48` — `extension_error(session_id: &str, kind: &str, ...)` takes two adjacent `&str` parameters with different meanings; swapping them compiles and would emit a JSON error payload with the session id in the `error` field and the kind in `sessionId`, breaking the machine-readable branch the client takes on `data.error`. Make the kind parameter a dedicated type so the positions can't be swapped — e.g. a `&'static str`-wrapping `ErrorKind` newtype, or accept the kind constants through a small enum that maps to the shared contract strings.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/session_fork.rs:351` — `test_cwd` is a needless helper: it wraps the single expression `PathBuf::from("/tmp")` and has exactly one call site (`new_session` at line 400). The doc comment's stated purpose ("named once so a future change edits a single place") is moot with one caller — the one caller IS the single place. Inline `PathBuf::from("/tmp")` into `new_session` and delete `test_cwd`; reintroduce a helper only when a second caller or a per-test TempDir actually arrives.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/session_fork.rs:365` — `batch_size: 512` is a hardcoded buffer/batch size in the newly added test `agent_config`. It configures model behaviour with no explanation, and duplicates the same literal already hardcoded in `create_test_model_config` in crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs — two unexplained copies that can drift. Name the value (e.g. `const TEST_BATCH_SIZE: u32 = 512;`) or, better, reuse one shared model-free test `ModelConfig` builder (the new `test_utils.rs` is the natural home) instead of repeating the literal config block per test module.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/session_fork.rs:435` — The seeded KV blob size `64` in `primed_parent` (`vec![0xAB; 64]`) configures test behaviour and is silently coupled, across functions, to the assertion `assert_eq!(status.bytes, Some(64))` at line 661. If the seed size changes, the distant assertion breaks with no signpost connecting the two. Introduce a named constant in the test module, e.g. `const SEEDED_STATE_BYTES: usize = 64;`, use it in `primed_parent` (`vec![0xAB; SEEDED_STATE_BYTES]`) and in the assertion (`Some(SEEDED_STATE_BYTES as u64)`), so the seed size and its assertion stay linked.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/test_utils.rs:104` — `StateDirGuard` is a public type with a non-empty representation but no `Debug` impl. The module is compiled into non-test builds so integration tests can import it, which makes this part of the crate's public surface — downstream test code cannot `dbg!()` or `assert!(..., "{guard:?}")` with it, and the orphan rule prevents adding `Debug` from outside. Add `#[derive(Debug)]` to `StateDirGuard` (both `TempDir` and `Option<OsString>` are `Debug`).
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/acp_integration.rs:40` — Same hardcoded `batch_size: 512` in the other module's `build_server` helper — a second copy of the unnamed buffer size, doubling the drift risk. Use `..ModelConfig::default()` here as well, or share one named constant/helper between the two `build_server` functions.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/acp_integration.rs:978` — `PathBuf::from("/tmp")` is repeated 7 times in the new `session_resume_tests` module (10 times in the file) as the session cwd. The sibling test module added in this same diff already names this exact literal (`test_cwd()` in session_fork.rs) precisely so a future change — e.g. a per-test TempDir — edits one place; this module didn't follow its own pattern. Add the same `fn test_cwd() -> PathBuf { PathBuf::from("/tmp") }` helper to `session_resume_tests` (or hoist it into `acp::test_utils` alongside the server builders) and use it at every call site.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/real_model_helpers.rs:35` — Hardcoded batch size `64` configures model decode behaviour with no explanation of why this value was chosen; per the validator note, size/configuration literals are still magic numbers inside test helpers. Extract a module-level `const TEST_MODEL_BATCH_SIZE: u32 = 64;` (with a one-line rationale, e.g. small enough for the 0.6B test model) and use it in the struct literal.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/real_model_helpers.rs:37` — The retry policy is hardcoded inline (`max_retries: 2`, `initial_delay_ms: 100`, `backoff_multiplier: 1.5`, `max_delay_ms: 1000`); retry counts and timeout/backoff values are exactly the configuration literals this validator targets, and nothing explains why these values suit the HuggingFace download path. Hoist the values into named constants (e.g. `const MODEL_DOWNLOAD_MAX_RETRIES: u32 = 2;`, `const MODEL_DOWNLOAD_INITIAL_DELAY_MS: u64 = 100;`, etc.) or a `const`-constructed `TEST_RETRY_CONFIG`, with a comment on why two quick retries are enough for the test model fetch.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/real_model_helpers.rs:45` — Thread counts `n_threads: 4` / `n_threads_batch: 4` are hardcoded concurrency limits with no rationale; if the value matters (CI core count, determinism) it deserves a named constant, and if it doesn't, the implicit coupling between the two `4`s is invisible. Introduce `const TEST_MODEL_THREADS: i32 = 4;` and assign it to both fields so the shared value is explicit and changeable in one place.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/real_model_helpers.rs:109` — `text_prompt` is now the second in-crate copy of the single-text-block `PromptRequest` constructor — `acp/server.rs::tests::hook_lifecycle::text_prompt` is a 0.96 match in the same crate. Both compilation units can already import `llama_agent::acp::test_utils` (this diff uses its `StateDirGuard` from integration tests), so one shared copy is reachable from both sites. Move `text_prompt` into `llama_agent::acp::test_utils` and have both the `hook_lifecycle` unit tests and the real-model integration tests call that one copy.
